phenomenon: rapid social inference from facial appearance
claim: Face evaluation reduces to two orthogonal dimensions (valence/dominance) that overgeneralize adaptive threat-inference mechanisms.

phenomenon: subjective perception of truthfulness from language presentation
claim: Concrete language increases perceived truth through heightened vividness, especially when aligned with the observer's cognitive construal level.
Political ideology correlates with physiological responsiveness to threat, suggesting biological grounding for policy preferences toward structural protection.
Dishonest behavior stems from automatic self-interest; ethical restraint requires both ample deliberation time and absence of available justifications.
